# Format:

#
# mode:
#   enforce  hook fails if a modified file defines this name outside owner-path

# architecture.

LuaStatus       enum    crates/lua-types/src/status.rs   enforce  canonical status enum

content.text:
Clean build. Summary:

**Files modified:**
- `crates/lua-parse/Cargo.toml` — added `lua-lex` dependency.
- `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:565-570` — added `source: &[u8]` to `ParserHook` signature.
- `crates/lua-vm/src/do_.rs:41-67` — `parse_stub` now drains the `ZIO` into a `Vec<u8>` (prepending `firstchar`) and passes the source bytes to the hook.
- `crates/lua-cli/src/main.rs:25-39` — `parser_hook` accepts and forwards `source`.
- `crates/lua-lex/src/lib.rs` — added `ZIO::new` and `ZIO::from_bytes` constructors; promoted `init`, `set_input`, `next`, `lookahead` from `pub(crate)` to `pub` so the parse crate can call them.
- `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:2732-2820` — `parse` now accepts `source`, builds a `lua_lex::ZIO::from_bytes`, constructs a `lua_lex::LexState` with the interned source/`_ENV` names, calls `lua_lex::next` to pull the first token, copies it into the local `LexState` stub (via a new `local_token_value` helper bridging the `lua_lex::TokenValue` enum to the parser's flat `TokenValue` record), and returns a `Box::new(LuaProto::placeholder())`.

**What this now does:** The parse function actually drives the lexer for the first token instead of returning a stub Err. `load_string` will return status 0 (success). The placeholder `LuaProto` has no bytecode, so the next iteration will surface whatever todo!() the VM hits when `pcall_k` tries to execute an empty proto.

**Downstream todo!() left for next iterations:** `mainfunc → statlist → …` and all 30+ `// TODO(port): lua_lex::next` sites in the parser body are untouched; whatever VM call panics on the empty proto is the next loop target.

**Build status:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green (warnings only, no errors).

Clean build. Summary:

**Files modified:**
- `crates/lua-parse/Cargo.toml` — added `lua-lex` dependency.
- `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:565-570` — added `source: &[u8]` to `ParserHook` signature.
- `crates/lua-vm/src/do_.rs:41-67` — `parse_stub` now drains the `ZIO` into a `Vec<u8>` (prepending `firstchar`) and passes the source bytes to the hook.
- `crates/lua-cli/src/main.rs:25-39` — `parser_hook` accepts and forwards `source`.
- `crates/lua-lex/src/lib.rs` — added `ZIO::new` and `ZIO::from_bytes` constructors; promoted `init`, `set_input`, `next`, `lookahead` from `pub(crate)` to `pub` so the parse crate can call them.
- `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:2732-2820` — `parse` now accepts `source`, builds a `lua_lex::ZIO::from_bytes`, constructs a `lua_lex::LexState` with the interned source/`_ENV` names, calls `lua_lex::next` to pull the first token, copies it into the local `LexState` stub (via a new `local_token_value` helper bridging the `lua_lex::TokenValue` enum to the parser's flat `TokenValue` record), and returns a `Box::new(LuaProto::placeholder())`.

**What this now does:** The parse function actually drives the lexer for the first token instead of returning a stub Err. `load_string` will return status 0 (success). The placeholder `LuaProto` has no bytecode, so the next iteration will surface whatever todo!() the VM hits when `pcall_k` tries to execute an empty proto.

**Downstream todo!() left for next iterations:** `mainfunc → statlist → …` and all 30+ `// TODO(port): lua_lex::next` sites in the parser body are untouched; whatever VM call panics on the empty proto is the next loop target.

**Build status:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green (warnings only, no errors).
